title: Compression-release breakout persistence
plain_language_summary_zh: 研究窄幅压缩后的突破是否比普通区间突破更具方向持续性。
falsifiable_hypothesis: Breakouts following a pre-declared 1h range-compression state persist longer than breakouts without prior compression on approved Development data.
proposed_market_mechanism: Order accumulation during compression may produce a directional liquidity release when the range boundary breaks.
supporting_evidence: The approved scope permits descriptive 1h analysis with 4h context.
contradictory_evidence: Compression boundaries can attract stop-driven false breakouts.

source_refs.claim: The frozen state supplies the approved descriptive scope and runtime bindings.

novelty_vs_existing_research: Tests the compression precondition separately from generic breakout labels.
minimal_test_method: Compare fixed-horizon directional persistence for compressed and non-compressed boundary breaks.
comparison_baseline: Boundary breaks without the pre-declared compression state.
falsification_conditions: Compression-conditioned breaks do not persist more often on either approved pair.
stop_conditions: Stop after the fixed threshold matrix; do not tune compression thresholds.
known_limitations: Boundary definitions can be sensitive to the selected fixed lookback.
